findings.claim: The experiment assumes that the selected Transformer model (SciBERT) is the optimal choice for domain adaptation.
findings.failure_mechanism: Other Transformer models (e.g., BioBERT, ClinicalBERT) might perform differently.
findings.evidence_test: Compare performance across multiple domain-adapted Transformers.

findings.claim: The PoC assumes that report-level recall is the most clinically relevant metric.
findings.failure_mechanism: Other metrics (e.g., span-level F1) might better capture clinical utility.
findings.evidence_test: Consult with clinicians to validate the chosen metric.

findings.claim: The experiment does not explicitly test the impact of annotation quality on model performance.
findings.failure_mechanism: Poor annotation quality could affect model reliability.
findings.evidence_test: Analyze performance on subsets with varying annotation quality.

critic_r1_output.summary: The proof-of-concept is methodologically sound with appropriate controls and evaluation metrics. No major flaws were identified.

adjudicator_output.case_rationale: no advancing findings from critic R1
